title: Greetings & Goodbyes
shortSummary: Greet people, introduce yourself, and say goodbye in German, the foundation of every conversation.
culturalNote: Germans value a clear, firm greeting. A handshake is standard when meeting someone, even in casual settings, and good eye contact signals respect. The big social decision is Sie versus du: use the formal Sie with strangers, older people, and in professional life until you are invited to switch to the informal du. Regional greetings vary: you will hear Moin in the north, Servus in Bavaria and Austria, and Gruezi in Switzerland.

keyPhrases.context: Universal, works in almost any situation

grammar.explanation: German has two words for 'you': Sie (formal, always capitalised) and du (informal). Use Sie with strangers, older people, and at work; use du with friends, family, and children.
grammar.commonMistake: Using du with a stranger or an older person, which can sound too familiar or even rude.

grammar.explanation: German greetings change with the time: Guten Morgen in the morning, Guten Tag during the day, Guten Abend in the evening. Gute Nacht is only for going to bed.
grammar.commonMistake: Saying Gute Nacht as an evening greeting. It only means 'good night' when leaving to sleep.

dialogue.setup: Frau Weber meets her new colleague Herr Klein in the office on his first morning.

lines.speaker: Herr Klein
lines.german: Guten Morgen! Wie geht es Ihnen?

lines.speaker: Frau Weber
lines.english: Very well, thank you. And you?

lines.speaker: Herr Klein
lines.german: Auch gut, danke. Ich bin Herr Klein, der neue Kollege.
lines.english: Also well, thanks. I'm Mr Klein, the new colleague.

lines.speaker: Frau Weber
lines.german: Freut mich! Ich bin Frau Weber.
lines.english: Nice to meet you! I'm Mrs Weber.

lines.speaker: Herr Klein
lines.english: Nice to meet you too. See you later!

lines.speaker: Frau Weber
lines.english: See you later! Have a nice day!

quiz.question: How do you say 'Good evening' in German?
quiz.explanation: Guten Abend is used from the early evening onward.

quiz.question: Which greeting is formal?
quiz.explanation: Ihnen is the formal form. 'Wie geht's?' is the casual short version.

quiz.question: Guten _____ (greeting used before 11am)
quiz.explanation: Guten Morgen is the morning greeting.

quiz.question: Which word means a casual 'bye'?
quiz.explanation: Tschüss is the everyday informal farewell; Auf Wiedersehen is formal.

quiz.question: Arrange: geht / es / mir / gut
quiz.explanation: The reply puts mir first, then the verb geht in second position.
